title: hoya fetuana subsp. tutuilensis
intro: Hoya fetuana subsp. tutuilensis, also known as Tutuila Hoya, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, native to the Pacific Islands. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Hoya fetuana subsp. tutuilensis are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing. The plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture for medicinal purposes, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and benefits.
